title: Create and prepare VK Odnoklassniki and Facebook pages
description: Child task for omnichannel category subscriptions.
acceptance_criteria: VK, Odnoklassniki, and Facebook pages exist, have profile/cover branding, starter descriptions, links back to 1001sovet.ru, and are linked from the subscription flow.

title: Cover omnichannel subscription flows with tests
description: Child task for omnichannel category subscriptions.
acceptance_criteria: Tests cover category selection, every direct channel, social-page links, opt-out, duplicate suppression, provider failure, and delivery retries.

title: Add subscription audit trail and operator visibility
description: Child task for omnichannel category subscriptions.
acceptance_criteria: Admins can inspect subscriptions, source, channel status, provider readiness, delivery history, failures, suppressed contacts, dry-runs, and test sends.

title: Add VK Odnoklassniki and Facebook social follow targets
description: Child task for omnichannel category subscriptions.
acceptance_criteria: Social pages are surfaced as first-class follow targets, clearly separated from direct message channels, and CTA clicks are tracked.

title: Implement delivery pipeline for all direct channels
description: Child task for omnichannel category subscriptions.
acceptance_criteria: A digest job fans out to all opted-in confirmed direct channels with idempotency, retries, and per-channel failure isolation.

title: Build subscription UI for category and channel selection
description: Child task for omnichannel category subscriptions.
acceptance_criteria: Users select categories and channels in one flow, edit choices later, and see per-channel confirmation/status/errors without dead ends.

title: Define omnichannel subscription data model and preference rules
description: Child task for omnichannel category subscriptions.
acceptance_criteria: Stores recipients, selected categories, per-channel opt-in/status, social follow targets, consent ledger, delivery ledger, and suppression.

title: Omnichannel category subscriptions for selected categories
description: Users subscribe to selected categories and choose delivery channels from day one: email, Telegram, MAX, WhatsApp, SMS/phone, plus VK/Odnoklassniki/Facebook social follow targets.
acceptance_criteria: Users can select categories, choose channels, confirm/manage/unsubscribe per channel, and social follow targets are visible and tracked.
notes:
Implementation plan saved at docs/superpowers/plans/2026-06-02-omnichannel-category-subscriptions.md. Initial execution completed Task 1 validation/constants, Task 2 Supabase migration, and Task 3 publication index scripts/tests; verification: npm run lint, npm test, npm run build.
Continued implementation: added subscriptions Worker scaffold with durable /subscriptions/start writes, persistent Supabase rate limits, confirmation handling, diagnostics and social tracking; added static /podpiski page, category/article CTAs, footer replacement, social click tracking component, provider setup checklist. Verification: npm run lint, npm test, npm run build, wrangler dry-run, HTTP static checks on /podpiski, /kulinaria, article route.
2026-06-02 implementation update: completed first working pass for omnichannel category subscriptions. Added category-bound UI and /podpiski flow, manage_token mode, unsubscribe_token flow, Supabase schema for recipients/contacts/topics/preferences/confirmations/consents/publication index/deliveries/suppression/social targets/rate limits, subscription Worker with /subscriptions/start, /confirm, /manage, /unsubscribe, Telegram/MAX/WhatsApp/Resend webhooks, admin diagnostics/dry-run/test-send behind ADMIN_API_KEY, scheduled digest processor, provider adapters for email/Telegram/MAX/WhatsApp/SMS, social follow targets/tracking, article publication index sync scripts, ops checklist. Verified npm run lint, npm test, npm run build, wrangler dry-run, and in-app browser QA for /podpiski, /kulinaria, article CTA, manage mode.
2026-06-02 review fixes: addressed Claude/Codex critical review set. Added fail-closed CORS/Turnstile behavior, ADMIN_API_KEY constant-time check, HMAC unsubscribe tokens with UNSUBSCRIBE_TOKEN_SECRET, keyed PII hashes, webhook secret/signature gates for Telegram/MAX/WhatsApp/Resend, non-mutating GET confirmation landing with POST confirm, duplicate contact recipient reuse, soft user unsubscribe, direct Worker List-Unsubscribe URL, retry-safe delivery semantics using sent-only dedup and failed-period retries, per-contact scheduled isolation, hourly timezone/delivery_window selection, MAX Authorization /messages shape, WhatsApp newline-free template params, social placeholder URL removal, manage-token hydration, shared validator usage, child-table RLS policies, atomic DB rate-limit RPC, handler-level tests via npx tsx. Verification: npm run lint, npm test, npm run build, wrangler dry-run, browser smoke for /podpiski and manage mode.
2026-06-02 Supabase apply: normal supabase db push was blocked because remote migration history contains older versions missing from local migrations. Applied subscription migrations directly to linked project sovetydoma/plwkjdpuxjkmpkqiqzkk via supabase db query --linked --file for 202606021300_omnichannel_subscriptions.sql and 202606021400_subscriptions_delivery_fairness.sql, then repaired those versions as applied. Verified remote notification tables, recipient manage/last_delivery columns, notification_check_rate_limit RPC, and social_follow_targets seeds. Remaining migration-history mismatch is older remote-only versions plus unrelated local pending analytics/view migrations.

title: Forensic codebase review 2026-06-02 completed - report in reports/forensic-codebase-review-2026-06-02.md
description: Thorough review performed: gates passed, security patterns strong, subscriptions partial, docs stale (PRODUCTION_PLAN), UGC rates missing, observability gap, key rotation needed. See report for full Reality Check, 10 risks, P0/P1/P2 plan.
